title: The Most Creative Posts From Pittsburgh Small Businesses
subtitle: Judged by Claude on visual concept, caption craft, format fit, and surprise. Not ranked by likes.
angle: the IG posts that took risks, made craft choices, or said something specific this quarter, ranked by editorial judgment instead of follower count.
intro:
Every quarter, we ask a simple question: which Pittsburgh small businesses are actually doing interesting things on Instagram? Not who has the most followers, not who boosted the most posts, not who hired an agency. A 14,000-follower bakery can outscore a 500,000-follower chain if the work is sharper. This list rewards craft: visual concept, caption writing, format intelligence, and the element of genuine surprise.

The top of this quarter's rankings belongs to three businesses tied at 8.3 out of 10. Page's landed there with an April Fools post that managed to be both technically impressive plating and genuinely funny, a combination harder to pull off than it sounds. Golden Age Beer Company stopped scrolls with vintage neon sign art, then wrote a caption that nerds out on Czech lager philosophy before cutting off mid-sentence, a move that somehow works. Lorelei made a neighborhood event feel like a cultural moment by naming the band, pricing the hot dogs, and using the word "glizzies" with complete confidence.

Issue 02 will bring a new set of posts, a few businesses we haven't seen before, and at least one surprise from a category you wouldn't expect.
